channel.title: Ofgem - Press releases
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for Ofgem press releases

item.title: Response to the Ofgem Review
item.description: Our response to the government's review of Ofgem.

item.title: Ofgem announces biggest shortlist of bidders since 2019 with offshore transmission assets for three major North Sea windfarms up for auction
item.description: Ofgem has today (Tuesday 24 March) announced it has shortlisted its biggest field of bidders since 2019.

item.title: Director resigns as energy supplier pays £525,000 for overcharging customers
item.description: A non-domestic energy supplier will pay £525,000 and its director has agreed to resign for serious and persistent mismanagement that led to customers being overcharged, Ofgem has confirmed today.

item.title: Ofgem secures £20 million redress payments from National Grid Electricity Transmission after licence failures at Harker 132kV substation
item.description: National Grid Electricity Transmission plc (NGET) has today (Wednesday 11th March 2026) agreed to pay £20 million into Ofgem’s Energy Industry Voluntary Redress Scheme.

item.title: Ofgem announces CEO Jonathan Brearley’s departure and transition plan
item.description: Ofgem Chief Executive Officer Jonathan Brearley will be stepping down from his role at the end of March to take up the position of Permanent Secretary at the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero.

item.title: Energy price cap will fall by 7% from April
item.description: The change amounts to a reduction of around £10 a month for the average household using both electricity and gas.

item.title: Ofgem to roll out tougher smart meter rules from February
item.description: New smart meter standards and more compensation for customers facing long wait times for installations or repairs will take effect from February, following a decision made by Ofgem.

item.title: OVO to pay £2.7 million in redress for Warm Home Discount payment failures
item.description: OVO will pay over £2.7 million in redress after failing to provide Warm Home Discount rebates to 11,646 customers by the statutory deadline of 31 March 2024.

item.title: Ofgem agrees early investment and updated dates for proposed electricity superhighways
item.description: Ofgem has approved updated delivery dates and early investment for three major projects which will help boost Great Britain’s energy security and clean power ambitions and deliver projected savings for consumers.

item.title: Ofgem sets out major reform package in next step to accelerate grid connections
item.description: Ofgem has unveiled proposals to speed up grid connections, whilst at the same time warning network operators to hit tough new targets or risk facing financial penalties.

item.title: Ofgem unlocks £28 billion investment to maintain a safe, secure and resilient energy grid and to upgrade and expand capacity to meet growing demands
item.description: Network companies have been given the green light for multi-billion-pound funding to strengthen the stability, security and resilience of our energy networks.

item.title: Energy price cap will rise by 0.2% from January
item.description: This change amounts to an increase of around 28 pence a month for the average dual fuel household.

item.title: Ofgem unlocks early investment for slew of fast-track clean power projects
item.description: Ofgem approves early construction funding (ECF) for a number of major electricity transmission projects.

item.title: Ofgem appoints British Gas to take on customers of Tomato Energy
item.description: Ofgem has appointed British Gas to take on supplying Tomato Energy's 15,000 domestic customers, and 8,000 non-domestic customers.

item.title: Ofgem unveils plans for overhaul of energy customer service standards
item.description: Ofgem is exploring regulatory reforms to streamline the rulebook and create a more agile energy sector.

item.title: Ofgem protects customers of Tomato Energy
item.description: Tomato Energy has today announced it is ceasing to trade. Customers’ energy supply will continue and funds that domestic customers have paid into their accounts, including existing credit balances, will be protected.

item.title: Ofgem and energy suppliers encourage consumers to reclaim £240 million in closed account balances
item.description: Around £240m of unclaimed credit balances in closed accounts are waiting to be returned to consumers.

item.title: Ofgem to set out plan to ‘reset and reform’ growing energy debt
item.description: A scheme to tackle historical debt built up during the energy crisis could help around 195,000 customers by writing off up to £500m, Ofgem announced today.

item.title: Ofgem confirms plans to introduce lower standing charge tariffs
item.description: Ofgem announces a new requirement for suppliers to offer at least one lower standing charge tariff to their customers.

item.title: Super battery projects that maximise renewable-generated power enter next phase of Ofgem’s green power storage scheme
item.description: Ofgem confirms the projects entering the final assessment stage of a government-driven ‘super battery’ support scheme.
